evals.name: capture-debugging-gotcha
evals.prompt: That was tricky — we just spent 20 minutes figuring out that the PostgreSQL connection pool was exhausting because we forgot to close cursors in the async context manager. The fix was adding `async with` instead of manual `cursor()` calls in `src/db/queries.py`. Let's capture this learning.
evals.expected_output: A markdown doc in docs/solutions/gotchas/ capturing the PostgreSQL cursor leak issue with frontmatter, Problem, Insight, Solution, and Prevention sections
expectations: Spawns a Task subagent (haiku) to extract the learning from conversation context before writing any files
The subagent prompt asks for TITLE, CATEGORY, WHAT HAPPENED, WHY IT MATTERS, THE INSIGHT, CODE EXAMPLES, PREVENTION/REUSE, TAGS, RELATED FILES, and COMPONENT
Searches docs/ directory with Grep for existing related docs before creating a new file
Creates the file under docs/solutions/gotchas/ matching the gotchas category directory mapping
Filename follows the slugified-title-YYYYMMDD.md pattern
Written doc includes YAML frontmatter with title, date, category, tags, component, severity, and status fields
Written doc contains Problem, Insight, and Solution sections with concrete details from the conversation
Presents final output in the format: Done — docs/solutions/gotchas/<filename>.md followed by a summary and numbered next steps
anti_expectations: The subagent does not write any files — only the main conversation writes files
Does not use a generic title like 'Fixed a bug' or 'Database issue'
Does not include filler phrases like 'It's important to note that' or 'This approach ensures'

evals.name: capture-critical-architecture-decision
evals.prompt: Document this — we decided to use event sourcing instead of CRUD for the audit log system because we need full replay capability and the write volume is 50x reads. This is a critical architectural decision that affects the entire harness pipeline. /learn architecture decision on event sourcing for audit logs
evals.expected_output: A markdown doc in docs/solutions/architecture/ with high/critical severity, and a one-liner appended to CLAUDE.md under Prior Learnings
expectations: Spawns a Task subagent (haiku) to extract the learning before writing files
Searches docs/ for existing related documentation on similar topics
Creates the file under docs/solutions/architecture/ matching the architecture category
Written doc includes YAML frontmatter with severity set to high or critical
Written doc emphasizes the Insight section with trade-off analysis as recommended for architecture docs
Appends a one-liner to the Critical gotchas list in CLAUDE.md because severity is high or critical
The CLAUDE.md entry follows the format: - `<title>` — see `docs/solutions/architecture/<filename>.md`
Runs mkdir -p docs/solutions/architecture/ before writing the file
anti_expectations: Does not skip the CLAUDE.md update for high/critical severity learnings
Does not have the subagent write files directly
Does not produce a doc where the Insight section just restates the Solution

evals.name: capture-design-pattern-with-existing-docs
evals.prompt: Let's compound this — we just implemented a retry-with-backoff pattern for all external API calls using a decorator. The pattern uses exponential backoff with jitter, maxing at 3 retries. We applied it to the OpenAI, Anthropic, and Stripe integrations in src/integrations/. Capture this as a reusable design pattern.
evals.expected_output: A markdown doc in docs/solutions/design-patterns/ with code examples showing the decorator pattern, cross-referenced with any existing related docs
expectations: Spawns a Task subagent (haiku) to extract the learning from conversation context
Searches docs/ for existing docs related to retry, backoff, or API integration patterns
Makes an explicit decision about whether to create new doc, update existing doc, or cross-reference based on search results
Creates the file under docs/solutions/design-patterns/ matching the design-patterns category
Written doc includes code examples with file paths as recommended for design pattern docs
Written doc includes a Prevention/Reuse section with concrete checklist items, not vague advice
Tags in frontmatter are lowercase hyphenated keywords relevant to the pattern for searchability
Presents the Done message with a 2-sentence summary and numbered next steps (View, Cross-reference, Continue)
anti_expectations: Does not skip searching docs/ for existing related documentation
Does not write Prevention section with vague advice like 'be careful' instead of concrete checks
Does not produce code examples without file paths
